table.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top;

tr.style: border: 1px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

a.title: The logical name of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

a.title: Information about the use of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

a.title: Minimum and Maximum # of times the element can appear in the instance

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px; width: 100px

a.title: Reference to the type of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck1.png)

a.title:
Ancestor of all Party types, including real world entities and their roles. A Party is any entity which can participate in an activity. The `_name_` attribute inherited from `LOCATABLE` is used to indicate the actual type of party (note that the actual names, i.e. identities of parties are indicated in the `_identities_` attribute, not the `_name_` attribute).
NOTE: It is strongly recommended that the inherited attribute `_uid_` be populated in `PARTY` objects, using the UID copied from the `_object_id()_` of the `_uid_` field of the enclosing `VERSION` object. + For example, the `ORIGINAL_VERSION.uid` `87284370-2D4B-4e3d-A3F3-F303D2F4F34B::uk.nhs.ehr1::2` would be copied to the `_uid_` field of the `PARTY` object.

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

a.style: padding-left: 3px; padding-right: 3px; border: 1px maroon solid; font-weight: bold; color: #301212; background-color: #fdf4f4;; padding-left: 3px; padding-right: 3px; border: 1px maroon solid; font-weight: bold; color: #301212; background-color: #fdf4f4;
a.title: This element has or is affected by constraints ( Type_valid, Identities_valid, Contacts_valid, Relationships_validity, Reverse_relationships_validity, Is_archetype_root, Uid_mandatory )

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td:
This is an abstract type. Child types: ACTOR, ROLE
Ancestor of all Party types, including real world entities and their roles.
Elements defined in Ancestors:name, archetype_node_id, uid, links, archetype_details, feeder_audit
Constraints: Type_valid, Identities_valid, Contacts_valid, Relationships_validity, Reverse_relationships_validity, Is_archetype_root, Uid_mandatory

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck10.png)

a.title: Identities used by the party to identify itself, such as legal name, stage names, aliases, nicknames and so on.

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td: Identities used by the party to identify itself, such as legal name, stage names, aliases, nicknames and so on.

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck10.png)

vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck10.png)

a.title: All other details for this Party.

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td: All other details for this Party.

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck10.png)

a.title: References to relationships in which this Party takes part as target.

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td: References to relationships in which this Party takes part as target.

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck00.png)

a.title: Relationships in which this Party takes part as source.

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td: Relationships in which this Party takes part as source.

description.value: Type of party, such as `PERSON`, `ORGANISATION`, etc. Role name, e.g. general practitioner , nurse , private citizen . Taken from inherited `_name_` attribute.

Ancestor of all Party types, including real world entities and their roles. A Party is any entity which can participate in an activity. The `_name_` attribute inherited from `LOCATABLE` is used to indicate the actual type of party (note that the actual names, i.e. identities of parties are indicated in the `_identities_` attribute, not the `_name_` attribute).
NOTE: It is strongly recommended that the inherited attribute `_uid_` be populated in `PARTY` objects, using the UID copied from the `_object_id()_` of the `_uid_` field of the enclosing `VERSION` object. + For example, the `ORIGINAL_VERSION.uid` `87284370-2D4B-4e3d-A3F3-F303D2F4F34B::uk.nhs.ehr1::2` would be copied to the `_uid_` field of the `PARTY` object.

short.value: Ancestor of all Party types, including real world entities and their roles.

definition.value:
Ancestor of all Party types, including real world entities and their roles. A Party is any entity which can participate in an activity. The `_name_` attribute inherited from `LOCATABLE` is used to indicate the actual type of party (note that the actual names, i.e. identities of parties are indicated in the `_identities_` attribute, not the `_name_` attribute).
NOTE: It is strongly recommended that the inherited attribute `_uid_` be populated in `PARTY` objects, using the UID copied from the `_object_id()_` of the `_uid_` field of the enclosing `VERSION` object. + For example, the `ORIGINAL_VERSION.uid` `87284370-2D4B-4e3d-A3F3-F303D2F4F34B::uk.nhs.ehr1::2` would be copied to the `_uid_` field of the `PARTY` object.

human.value: Archetyped valid must satisfy exclusive conditions
Archetype node id valid must not be empty
Identities valid must not be empty

expression.value: relationships.exists() implies (relationships.empty().not() and then relationships.for_all (r | r.source = self)

human.value: Reverse relationships validity must be present

expression.value: reverse_relationships.exists() implies (reverse_relationships.empty().not() and then reverse_relationships.for_all (item | repository (\"demographics\").all_party_relationships.has_object (item) and then repository(\"demographics\").all_party_relationships.object (item).target = self))

short.value: Runtime name of this fragment, used to build runtime paths.

definition.value: Runtime name of this fragment, used to build runtime paths. This is the term provided via a clinical application or batch process to name this EHR construct: its retention in the EHR faithfully preserves the original label by which this entry was known to end users.

short.value: Design-time archetype identifier of this node taken from its generating archetype; used to build archetype paths.

definition.value:
Design-time archetype identifier of this node taken from its generating archetype; used to build archetype paths. Always in the form of an at-code, e.g. `at0005`. This value enables a 'standardised' name for this node to be generated, by referring to the generating archetype local terminology.
At an archetype root point, the value of this attribute is always the stringified form of the `_archetype_id_` found in the `_archetype_details_` object.

short.value: Optional globally unique object identifier for root points of archetyped structures.

definition.value: Optional globally unique object identifier for root points of archetyped structures.

short.value: Links to other archetyped structures (data whose root object inherits from `ARCHETYPED`, such as `ENTRY`, `SECTION` and so on).

definition.value: Links to other archetyped structures (data whose root object inherits from `ARCHETYPED`, such as `ENTRY`, `SECTION` and so on). Links may be to structures in other compositions.

short.value: Details of archetyping used on this node.

definition.value: Details of archetyping used on this node.

short.value: Audit trail from non-openEHR system of original commit of information forming the content of this node, or from a conversion gateway which has synthesised this node.

definition.value: Audit trail from non-openEHR system of original commit of information forming the content of this node, or from a conversion gateway which has synthesised this node.

short.value: Identities used by the party to identify itself, such as legal name, stage names, aliases, nicknames and so on.

definition.value: Identities used by the party to identify itself, such as legal name, stage names, aliases, nicknames and so on.

short.value: All other details for this Party.

definition.value: All other details for this Party.

short.value: References to relationships in which this Party takes part as target.

definition.value: References to relationships in which this Party takes part as target.

short.value: Relationships in which this Party takes part as source.

definition.value: Relationships in which this Party takes part as source.

short.value: Ancestor of all Party types, including real world entities and their roles.

definition.value:
Ancestor of all Party types, including real world entities and their roles. A Party is any entity which can participate in an activity. The `_name_` attribute inherited from `LOCATABLE` is used to indicate the actual type of party (note that the actual names, i.e. identities of parties are indicated in the `_identities_` attribute, not the `_name_` attribute).
NOTE: It is strongly recommended that the inherited attribute `_uid_` be populated in `PARTY` objects, using the UID copied from the `_object_id()_` of the `_uid_` field of the enclosing `VERSION` object. + For example, the `ORIGINAL_VERSION.uid` `87284370-2D4B-4e3d-A3F3-F303D2F4F34B::uk.nhs.ehr1::2` would be copied to the `_uid_` field of the `PARTY` object.

human.value: Identities valid must not be empty

expression.value: relationships.exists() implies (relationships.empty().not() and then relationships.for_all (r | r.source = self)

human.value: Reverse relationships validity must be present

expression.value: reverse_relationships.exists() implies (reverse_relationships.empty().not() and then reverse_relationships.for_all (item | repository (\"demographics\").all_party_relationships.has_object (item) and then repository(\"demographics\").all_party_relationships.object (item).target = self))

short.value: Identities used by the party to identify itself, such as legal name, stage names, aliases, nicknames and so on.

definition.value: Identities used by the party to identify itself, such as legal name, stage names, aliases, nicknames and so on.

short.value: All other details for this Party.

definition.value: All other details for this Party.

short.value: References to relationships in which this Party takes part as target.

definition.value: References to relationships in which this Party takes part as target.

short.value: Relationships in which this Party takes part as source.

definition.value: Relationships in which this Party takes part as source.
